action.reasoning: Observation: best full-test exploration_score=0.6057 (success_rate=0.8733, travel~338m on 150 maps; rooms weakest ~0.8 success). Recent runs stuck on proxy (optimistic ~0.83 on 9 easy maps) or 0.0 due to predict.py truncation in run_evaluation (workspace cutoff at base=Path). Analysis: current Dijkstra-based global heuristic (utility**beta)/(dist**alpha+1) + next-hop stepping on provided node graph is strong standard frontier planner (0 params, fully compatible with real ExplorationEnv, node utilities, eval contract, <=50M). Root cause=long code causing repeated truncation + fragile fallback. New alpha=2.0/beta=1.8 emphasizes high-utility frontier clusters more (helps rooms) while controlling distance. Decision: overwrite with COMPACT COMPLETE non-truncated predict.py (cleaned select_waypoint+coord handling, single dijkstra call, robust eval_config.json parse OR glob fallback to 3 maps/type for 9-map proxy, per-episode try/except, exact JSON contract with per_episode list + per_type (success-only dist) + correct (1/N)*sum(S_i*300/d_i), proxy scoring). Update train.py with new params+v15 desc. Run train+proxy validation then submit_eval for official 150-map test score.
action.command: python train.py && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline --output output.json
